coredata.dc:title: One-pot electrodeposition of metal organic frameworks composite accelerated by gold nanoparticles and electroreduced carbon dots for electroanalysis of bisphenol A in real plastic samples
coredata.dc:creator: Zhan, Xuhua
Hu, Shiyu
Wang, Jiaqi
Chen, Hong
Chen, Ximing
Yang, Jianbo
Yang, Hai
Su, Zhaohong
coredata.dc:description:
A sensitive electroanalysis of bisphenol A (BPA) based on a novel metal organic frameworks (MOFs) composite of MOFs, gold nanoparticles (AuNPs) and electroreduced carbon dots (ErCDs) was reported. The MOFs composite (AuNPs-ErCDs-MOFs) was in-situ prepared by one-pot electrodeposition approach. Scanning electron microscopy (SEM), energy dispersive spectroscopy (EDS), Fourier transform infrared spectrometer (FT-IR), cyclic voltammetry (CV), electrochemical impedance spectroscopy (EIS) and differential pulse voltammetry (DPV) were used to show the properties of the composite. Interestingly, AuNPs, ErCDs, and AuNPs-ErCDs are found to significantly accelerate electrodeposition of MOFs, and used as good conductive agent to enhance the conductivity of MOFs composite. The electrochemical sensor shows wide linear range from 7.0 × 10−8 mol/L to 5 × 10 −
7 mol/L and 5 × 10−7 mol/L to 1.3 × 10−6 mol/L for BPA with a detection limit of 32 nmol/L (S/N = 3). In addition, the proposed sensor was also applied to test the BPA in plastic products with satisfactory recovery.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Metal organic frameworks composite
Gold nanoparticles
Electroreduced carbon dots
One-Pot electrodeposition
Bisphenol A electroanalysis

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: Detection of BPA in actual samples

xocs:funding-list.xocs:funding-text: This work was supported by the National Natural Science Foundation of China ( 21305039 ), the Natural Science Foundation of Hunan Province ( 2020JJ4346 , 14JJ3097 ), the Foundation of Department of Education of Hunan Province ( 20K063 ), Degree & Postgraduate Education Reform Project of Hunan Province ( 2019JGYB130 ), Hunan Natural Science Foundation for Distinguished Young Scholars ( 2021JJ10001 ), Hunan Science and Technology Talent Promotion Project ( 2020TJ-Q12 ), the Provincial Innovation and Entrepreneurship Training Program for College Students ( S201910537026 ). Xuhua Zhan and Shiyu Hu contributed equally to this work.

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: SEM images of MOFs (A), AuNPs-MOFs (B), ErCDs-MOFs (C), AuNPs-ErCDs-MOFs (D and E) on the GCE, respectively. Elemental mapping distribution of AuNPs-ErCDs-MOFs (F-J) for CK, OK, ZnK, AuK, NK, respectively. EDX spectrum of AuNPs-ErCDs-MOFs (K). Electrodeposition potential: E = −1.3 V vs. SCE., electrodeposition time t = 200 s.
FT-IR (A) of MOFs (a), ErCDs-MOFs (b), AuNPs-MOFs (c), and AuNPs-ErCDs-MOFs (d). XRD (B) patterns of MOFs, AuNPs-MOFs, ErCDs-MOFs, and AuNPs- ErCDs-MOFs.
TEM (A-D) and HRTEM (inset) images of AuNPs-ErCDs-MOFs composite.
XPS survey spectrum of AuNPs-ErCDs-MOFs composite (A), high resolution XPS spectrum of Zn2p (B), O1s (C), Au4f (D), C1s (E), N1s (F), respectively.

CV (A) and EIS (B) of GCE, MOFs/GCE, AuNPs-MOFs/GCE, ErCDs-MOFs/GCE and AuNPs-ErCDs-MOFs/GCE in 5.0 mM [Fe(CN)6]3−/4− + 0.5 M KCl solution. Scan rate of 100 mV/s. EIS parameters: 100 kHz to 1 Hz, 5 mV rms, 0.20 V vs. SCE.

Effects of the electrodeposition time (A), preconcentration time (B), pH of PBS (C), concentration of AuNPs (D) and concentration of CDs (E). DPV (F) of GCE, MOFs/GCE, AuNPs-MOFs/GCE, ErCDs-MOFs/GCE, AuNPs-ErCDs-MOFs/GCE in PBS (pH = 7) containing 50 μM BPA.
(A) DPV response of AuNPs-ErCDs-MOFs/GCE to different BPA concentration, and (B) the linear relationship between DPV peak current and BPA concentration.

(A) DPV responses of AuNPs-ErCDs-MOFs/GCE in 0.1 M PBS (pH = 7) containing 50 μM BPA and different interferences (5000 μM of KCl, MgSO4, NaCl, Zn(NO3)2 and 500 μM of catechol, hydroquinone, phenol), respectively.
One pot preparation of AuNPs-ErCDs-MOFs composite and construction of BPA electrochemical sensor.
The results of EDX (Fig. 1K) for AuNPs-ErCDs-MOFs composite.
*Comparison of different modified electrodes for BPA detection.

ce:table-footnote.ce:note-para: ILs-LDH: ionic liquids functionalized layered double hydroxide, CB/f-MWCNTs: carboxylic acid functionalized carbon black-multi-walled carbon nanotubes, CTpPa-2: covalent organic framework CTpPa-2, CTAB: cetyltrimethylammonium bromide, RGO: reduced graphene oxide, PLL: poly-l-lysine, BCNPs: biochar nanoparticle, Tyr: tyrosinase enzyme, CPE: carbon paste electrode.

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: *Determination of BPA in the actual sample. (n = 3).

ce:table-footnote.ce:note-para: 1: PET mineral water bottle, 2: PP disposable plastic cup, 3: PP disposable lunch box, 4: Plastic food packaging bags.

head.ce:title: One-pot electrodeposition of metal organic frameworks composite accelerated by gold nanoparticles and electroreduced carbon dots for electroanalysis of bisphenol A in real plastic samples

ce:affiliation.ce:label: a
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: College of Chemistry and Materials Science, Hunan Agricultural University, Changsha, 410128, PR China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: College of Chemistry and Materials Science

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: College of Chemistry and Materials Science, Hunan Agricultural University, Changsha 410128, PR China

ce:affiliation.ce:label: b
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Hunan Provincial Key Laboratory of Environmental Catalysis & Waste Recycling, Hunan Institute of Engineering, Xiangtan, 411104, PR China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Hunan Provincial Key Laboratory of Environmental Catalysis & Waste Recycling

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Hunan Provincial Key Laboratory of Environmental Catalysis & Waste Recycling, Hunan Institute of Engineering, Xiangtan 411104, PR China

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para:
A sensitive electroanalysis of bisphenol A (BPA) based on a novel metal organic frameworks (MOFs) composite of MOFs, gold nanoparticles (AuNPs) and electroreduced carbon dots (ErCDs) was reported. The MOFs composite (AuNPs-ErCDs-MOFs) was in-situ prepared by one-pot electrodeposition approach. Scanning electron microscopy (SEM), energy dispersive spectroscopy (EDS), Fourier transform infrared spectrometer (FT-IR), cyclic voltammetry (CV), electrochemical impedance spectroscopy (EIS) and differential pulse voltammetry (DPV) were used to show the properties of the composite. Interestingly, AuNPs, ErCDs, and AuNPs-ErCDs are found to significantly accelerate electrodeposition of MOFs, and used as good conductive agent to enhance the conductivity of MOFs composite. The electrochemical sensor shows wide linear range from 7.0 × 10−8 mol/L to 5 × 10 −
7 mol/L and 5 × 10−7 mol/L to 1.3 × 10−6 mol/L for BPA with a detection limit of 32 nmol/L (S/N = 3). In addition, the proposed sensor was also applied to test the BPA in plastic products with satisfactory recovery.

ce:section.ce:para: As a potential endocrine disruptor, bisphenol A (BPA) has posed a great threat to environment and human [1]. Traditional BPA detection methods such as high-performance liquid chromatography and liquid chromatography tandem mass spectrometry can provide us with high precision and high automation [2]. However, the problems like the high cost, long detection time and complex sample pretreatment should also take into consideration. Therefore, it is necessary to establish a more effective, convenient, and sensitive method for BPA detection. Considering electrochemical method [3–5] has potential in solving the shortcomings of the above detection methods, and still has the advantages of high sensitivity and fast detection. It is gradually accepted and applied by more people. In recent years, the application of electrochemical sensing in the detection of electroactive substances has been more used.

Metal organic frameworks (MOFs) can easily connect metal ions and organic ligands through chemical bonds to form a crystal coordination polymer by chemical or electrochemical method [6,7]. It has attracted extensive attention in recent years, owing to its large specific surface area, abundant active sites and controllable structure [7]. However, considering the poor conductivity of most MOFs, its further application in the field of electrochemical sensing has been hindered. It has been reported that MOFs with different geometric frameworks [8] and different properties can be obtained by changing the types of metal ions or controlling the types of organic ligands, which can be explained by the abundant sites provided by guest materials. The methods of MOFs modification include using metal with oxidation or reduction as the center, introducing organic coordination linker with strong reactivity [9–11], or building MOFs composite material with good electrical conductivity [12–15]. These methods have provided good ideas for the development and application of MOFs. It is worth mentioning that CTAB/Ce-MOFs [16], Cu-MOFs/ErGO [17], Ce-MOFs-ErGO [18] and AuNPs@Cu-MOFs [19] can be used to electrochemical detection of BPA, however, all the related MOFs were prepared by conventional and reported chemical method, and the following MOFs composites were formed by multi-step reaction.

Carbon dots (CDs) has been widely recognized as a novel carbon material with ideal application prospects since it discovered [20,21] and has been widely used in detection and biological imaging, owing to its unique optical properties, good water solubility and biocompatibility [22,23]. However, CDs is less attractive in application of electrochemical sensors, one of the key problems is its poor conductivity. We believe that the electroreduced CDs (ErCDs) with improved conductivity and rich functional groups can make up for the limitations of their electrochemical sensors application. Meanwhile, ErCDs modified MOFs which prepared by one-pot electrodeposition can make up for the limitations of MOFs electrochemical sensing. However, to the best of our knowledge, such attempts have not been reported to date.

In this work, a novel MOFs composite including AuNPs and ErCDs is prepared by one-pot electrodeposition for amperometric detection of BPA. AuNPs, ErCDs, and AuNPs-ErCDs are used to accelerate electrodeposition of MOFs, and used as good conductive agent to enhance the conductivity of MOFs composite. The prepared amperometric sensor is found to exhibit good analytical properties (sensitivity, low detection limit) for BPA detection.

Sodium citrate, bisphenol A (BPA), catechol, hydroquinone, phenol and HAuCl4 were purchased from Sinopharm Chemical Reagent Co., Ltd. Urea was purchased from Beijing solebao Technology Co., Ltd. Zn(NO3)2 · 6H2O and KNO3 were purchased from Shandong West Asia Chemical Industry Co., Ltd. 98% of 1,3,5-benzoic acid (H3BTC) was purchased from Shanghai McLean Biochemical Technology Co., Ltd. Phosphate buffered saline (PBS, pH = 7.0) was prepared by mixing 0.1 M Na2HPO4 and 0.1 M NaH2PO4, and the pH was adjusted by HCl or NaOH. The experimental water was double-distilled deionized water.

All electrochemical experiments were conducted on a CHI660E electrochemical workstation (Shanghai Chenhua Instruments Co., Ltd., China) with a three-electrode system. Glassy carbon electrode (GCE) with diameter of 3 mm as the working electrode, KCl-saturated calomel electrode (SCE) as the reference electrode, and carbon rod as the counter electrode. And all potentials are referenced to the SCE in this work. The morphology and the EDX elemental analysis results of the composite were tested by scanning electron microscope (SEM, JEM-6700 F field emission scanning electron microscope). The TEM and High Resolution TEM (HRTEM) were collected from transmission electron microscope of Tecnai G2F20S-Twintwin (FEI company). Fourier transform infrared (FT-IR) spectra were recorded on a Nicolet Nexus 670 FT-IR spectrophotometer. X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy (XPS) was conducted on A K-Alpha+ instrument with Al Kα radiation (Thermo fisher Scientific, US). High performance liquid chromatography (HPLC) analysis was performed on an Agilent 1200 instrument.

CDs was prepared by pyrolysis. Briefly, 8.04 g urea and 4.04 g sodium citrate were weighted and put into a crucible, then dissolved by ultrapure water. Seal the crucible with tin foil and bake it at 160 °C for 4 h. After the initial filtration, the purified CDs was obtained by dialysis with a dialysis bag (3500 D) for 24 h. Finally, CDs powder was obtained by freeze-drying, and stored at 4 °C for later application.
The preparation of AuNPs [24] and treatment of GCE [5,25] were consistent with previous reports.

Preparation of AuNPs-ErCDs-MOFs composite modified electrode (Scheme 1 ). 0.262 g Zn(NO3)2·6H2O and 0.031 g KNO3 were weighed and dissolved in 3 mL ultrapure water (solution A). 0.105 g of H3BTC was dissolved in 3 mL absolute ethanol (solution B). After mixing solution A and solution B evenly, added 100 μL 0.01 mg/L CDs (optimized) and 60 μL 37.5 μg/L AuNPs (optimized), and stirred at room temperature for 24 h to mix MOFs precursor, AuNPs and CDs well. The three-electrode system was inserted into the above solution, and the modified electrode was prepared by one pot electroreduction at −1.3 V for 200 s. Rinse the electrode gently with water and dry under an infrared lamp. The dried modified electrode can be used for BPA detection, and other modified electrodes (MOFs/GCE, AuNPs-MOFs/GCE and ErCDs-MOFs/GCE) can also be obtained by this method.

Detection of BPA. DPV was used to detect BPA concentration at AuNPs-ErCDs-MOFs/GCE in the PBS. The optimization of detection conditions including electrodeposition time, pH of PBS, preconcentration time, and concentration of AuNPs.

Detection of BPA in plastic products. PET mineral water bottle, PP disposable plastic cup, PP disposable lunch box and food packaging bag were used to explore the content of BPA in actual samples. They were chopped and placed in a 70 °C water bath for 48 h. Appropriate amount of filtrate was added into PBS for BPA detection and analysis.

As shown in Fig. 2 A, FT-IR was used to characterize the composite. For MOFs [15], the vibration absorption peak of OH at about 3443 cm−1, the vibration absorption peak of CO at about 1635 cm −1, and the vibration absorption peak of COOH at about 1377 cm−1 were observed obviously. When the existence of ErCDs (Fig. 2b), the vibration absorption peak of 1377 cm−1 disappeared, and a small peak of 1358 cm−1 appeared, which may be the vibration absorption peak of CO. The peak position of Fig. 2c was roughly the same as that of Fig. 2a, indicating the addition of AuNPs has no obvious effect on the structure of MOFs. For (Fig. 2d), the stretching vibration peaks of OH and CO significantly increased, and the stretching vibration peaks of COOH significantly reduced, which indicate that the structure of composite can be improved by ErCDs and AuNPs. The crystal structures of MOFs, AuNPs-MOFs, ErCDs-MOFs and AuNPs-ErCDs-MOFs nanocomposites were studied by XRD (Fig. 2B). The diffraction peaks at 2θ = 30.2° and 35.1° are assigned to the crystal face (222) and (400) of ITO. The characteristic diffraction peak at 2θ = 38.2° is ascribed as Au (111) (JCPDS file No. 65-8601). The peak at 2θ = 27.8° is ascribed to ErCDs diffraction peak. The peaks at 2θ = 7.9°, 10.2°, 12.1° (d222), 24.1° and 26.1° are ascribed to the crystal face of MOFs [26,27], proving the formation of MOFs composite. The XRD patterns results demonstrate that the MOFs composite is fabricated successfully.

Fig. 3 shows the TEM (A–D) and HRTEM (inset) of AuNPs-ErCDs-MOFs composites. As shown in Fig. 3(A–D), AuNPs-ErCDs-MOFs composites present as columnar and needle like forms. Fig. 3D shows AuNPs, ErCDs are loaded on MOFs, the size of gold nanoparticles and ErCDs is about 20.1 nm and 3.4 nm, the inset is lattice spacing of gold nanoparticles. The results show that AuNPs-ErCDs-MOFs nanocomposites is prepared successfully.

The surface composition and chemical state of AuNPs-ErCDs-MOFs composite were examined by XPS analysis. XPS survey spectrum (Fig. 4 A) shows five prominent peaks at 1022.3 eV, 532.5 eV, 399.9 eV, 284.8 eV, 89.1 eV, which correspond to Zn2p, O1s, N1s, C1s, Au4f respectively. Fig. 4B shows Zn2p in the high resolution spectrum, two peaks [28] at 1045.4 eV and 1022.3 eV assigned to Zn2p1 and Zn2p3. Fig. 4C indicates the O1s spectrum is fitted by a series of components at 530.5 eV (OCO), 531.1 eV (CO), 531.7 eV (MO), 532.5 eV (CO), 533.5 eV (NCO). As shown in Fig. 4D (inset) is high resolution XPS spectrum of Au4f. two peaks are observed at 89.1 eV and 91.2 eV for Au4f5 and Au4f7, respectively, which could be assigned to the binding energy of Au (0) in the composite [29]. C1s spectrum of AuNPs-ErCDs-MOFs is deconvoluted into four different components located at 284.4 eV (C–C sp2), 284.8 eV (C–C sp3), 285.2 eV(COC), 288.9 eV (OCO), respectively. Fig. 4F indicates that N1s spectrum fitted component at binding energy of 349.5 eV, 398.7 eV, 399.9 eV, 401.7 eV are attributed to R2 CNCR, R2C NR, CNH2, CNH, respectively. C1s, O1s, N1s Fitting peaks assigned to ErCDs [30]. These results indicate that AuNPs, CDs, MOFs are at the distinct chemical state in AuNPs-ErCDs-MOFs composite.

The electrochemical properties of the modified composites were characterized in 0.5 M KCl +5.0 mM [Fe(CN)6]3−/4− solution, the CV and EIS of four different modified composites were obtained, as shown in Fig. 5 . According to Fig. 5, the bare GCE has a small peak-to-peak separation of anodic and cathodic peak potentials and a small diameter of the EIS semicircle, indicating its good electric conductivity. After the MOFs was added to the GCE, a significant decrease and increase were induced in the peak current and diameter of the EIS semicircle (MOFs/GCE), respectively. After ErCDs-MOFs, AuNPs-MOFs and AuNPs-ErCDs-MOFs composites were added onto the surface of the GCE, these structures induced the gradual increase and decrease of the peak current and the diameter of the EIS semicircle (ErCDs-MOFs/GCE, AuNPs-MOFs/GCE and AuNPs-ErCDs-MOFs/GCE), respectively. because the conducting AuNPs, ErCDs and AuNPs-ErCDs can somewhat recover the electrode activity which led to the peak current to further increase and the remarkable decrease in the resistance of the electrode interface. These results indicate that MOFs composite can be modified with AuNPs, ErCDs and AuNPs-ErCDs by electrodeposition technology. And AuNPs, ErCDs and AuNPs-ErCDs can effectively enhance the electronic transmission capacity of MOFs composite.

As shown in Fig. 6 F, the BPA amperometric responses of different modified electrodes were conducted by DPV. The detection solution was PBS (pH = 7.0) with 50 μM BPA. The scan rate was 50 mV/s, the enrichment time was 100 s, and the deposition time of electrode modification was 200 s. The peak potential of the modified electrodes to BPA was about 0.53 V with ErCDs, and the corresponding peak potential increased to 0.72 V without ErCDs. The peak currents of the modified electrodes satisfy the order AuNPs-ErCDs-MOFs/GCE > AuNPs-MOFs/GCE > ErCDs-MOFs/GCE > GCE > MOFs/GCE. So the AuNPs-ErCDs-MOFs/GCE is selected for BPA detection. The above results exhibit that the ErCDs can significantly impact the BPA peak potential and peak current at MOFs composite modified electrode by its specific properties.

To obtain good detection results, we also optimized the detection condition at AuNPs-ErCDs-MOFs/GCE. The optimization of electrodeposition time was shown in Fig. 6(A–E). The thickness of the composite for modified electrode was adjusted by changing the electrodeposition time at the potential of –1.3 V. The modified electrode was tested by DPV in PBS (pH = 7.0) containing 50 μM BPA, and the peak current was examined. The results show that the peak current increases before 200 s, and then gradually decreases. Therefore, 200 s is the best electrodeposition time. The optimization of preconcentration time was shown in Fig. 6B. The peak current was the highest when the preconcentration time was 100 s. Then the peak current decreases, which may be due to the passivation of the composite. Hence, 100 s is selected as the preconcentration time. The optimization of pH of PBS was shown in Fig. 6C. The peak current reached the maximum value at pH = 7, therefore, PBS with pH = 7 is selected as the supporting electrolyte. The optimization of the amount of AuNPs was shown in Fig. 6D. The results show that the peak current is the highest when adding 60 μL 37.5 μg/L AuNPs, and then decreases, which may be due to the agglomeration effect caused by excessive AuNPs. So, 37.5 μg/L is selected as optimum concentration of AuNPs. The optimization of the amount of CDs was shown in Fig. 6E. The results show that the peak current is the highest when adding 100 μL 0.01 mg/L CDs, and then decreases, which may be due to the agglomeration effect caused by excessive CDs. So, 0.01 mg/L is selected as optimum concentration of CDs.

Fig. 7 shows BPA detection at AuNPs-ErCDs-MOFs/GCE under optimal experimental condition (Fig. 6). Fig. 7A shows the DPV response of modified electrode toward BPA at different concentrations. Fig. 7B shows the linear relationship between peak current and BPA concentration. The linear regression equations are I (μA) = 1.84 × 103 c (nmol/L) + 0.36 (R2 = 0.9822) and I (μA) = 6.92 × 103 c (nmol/L) – 2.33 (R2 = 0.9966). The peak current of modified electrode had a linear correlation with BPA concentration in the range of 7 × 10−8 mol/L − 5 × 10−7 and 5 × 10−7 − 1.3 × 10−6 mol/L, and the limit of detection (LOD) was 32 nmol/L. The LOD is better than those reported at typical modified electrodes for BPA detection, as listed in Table 2 . The AuNPs-ErCDs-MOFs sensor was also investigated in the presence of 50 μM BPA and different concentration of some possible interfering substances (Fig. 8 ). The results show that 100-fold concentration of inorganic ions (K+, Mg2+, NO3 −, SO4 2−, Na+, Zn2+ and Cl−) and 10-fold concentration of catechol, hydroquinone and phenol, have no significant influence on the response of BPA. It shows that the sensor has good anti-interference ability.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Detection of BPA in actual samples
ce:section.ce:para: As shown in Table 3 , the standard addition method was used to determine the BPA concentration in mineral water bottle, plastic cup, lunch box and food packaging bag by AuNPs-ErCDs-MOFs/GCE. The results show that the recovery of BPA is in the range of 84.5%–100.9%, and the RSD (n = 3) ranged from 0.48% to 4.18%. HPLC was also used to further demonstrate the accuracy of the proposed electrode. The above results indicate that the proposed method was effective and accurate for practical applications.

In summary, a sensitive electroanalysis of BPA at AuNPs-ErCDs-MOFs/GCE was reported. The novel MOFs composite was in-suit prepared by one-pot electrodeposition. AuNPs, ErCDs, and AuNPs-ErCDs can significantly accelerate electrodeposition of MOFs proved by SEM, and used as good conductive agent to enhance the conductivity of MOFs composite explained by CV and EIS, which improve the performance of the sensor. When the proposed sensor was used to detect BPA, it had a wide linear range and a low LOD. It also had ideal recovery in the detection of actual samples, so it has potential application value in the field of BPA detection. In addition, the proposed method can be extended to green and rapid preparation of many other MOFs composites for wide applications.

Zhaohong Su and Hai Yang are the corresponding authors of this article. Xuhua Zhan, Shiyu Hu, Jiaqi Wang, Hong Chen, Ximing Chen, and Jianbo Yang have contributed to the development of the experimental part. Xuhua Zhan and Shiyu Hu have contributed to the corrections of the manuscript. Xuhua Zhan and Shiyu Hu contributed equally to this work.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: This work was supported by the National Natural Science Foundation of China (21305039), the Natural Science Foundation of Hunan Province (2020JJ4346, 14JJ3097), the Foundation of Department of Education of Hunan Province (20K063), Degree & Postgraduate Education Reform Project of Hunan Province (2019JGYB130), Hunan Natural Science Foundation for Distinguished Young Scholars (2021JJ10001), Hunan Science and Technology Talent Promotion Project (2020TJ-Q12), the Provincial Innovation and Entrepreneurship Training Program for College Students (S201910537026). Xuhua Zhan and Shiyu Hu contributed equally to this work.

sb:title.sb:maintitle: Electrochemical detection of bisphenols in food: a review

ce:bib-reference.ce:source-text: Y. Zhang, Y. Lei, H. Lu, L. Shi, P. Wang, Z. Ali, et al., Electrochemical detection of bisphenols in food: A review, Food Chem, 346 (2021) 128895. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.foodchem.2020.128895.

Yuan, Highly sensitive determination of tetrabromobisphenol A and bisphenol A in environmental water samples by solid‐phase extraction and liquid chromatography‐tandem mass spectrometry, J Sep Sci, 33 (2010) 1652-1657. https://doi.org/10.1002/jssc.201000010.

sb:title.sb:maintitle: A highly sensitive sensor based on a computer-designed magnetic molecularly imprinted membrane for the determination of acetaminophen

Xiao, A highly sensitive sensor based on a computer-designed magnetic molecularly imprinted membrane for the determination of acetaminophen, Biosens Bioelectron, 148 (2019) 111819-111826. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.bios.2019.111819

ce:biography.ce:simple-para: Xuhua Zhan is pursuing his B.Sc. degree in Material Chemistry in Hunan Agricultural University. His research interests include electroanalysis.
Shiyu Hu is pursuing his B.Sc. degree in Material Chemistry in Hunan Agricultural University. His research interests include electroanalysis.
Jiaqi Wang is pursuing her B.Sc. degree in Applied Chemistry in Hunan Agricultural University. Her research interests include electroanalysis.
Hong Chen is pursuing her B.Sc. degree in Applied Chemistry in Hunan Agricultural University. Her research interests include electroanalysis.
Ximing Chen is pursuing his B.Sc. degree in Applied Chemistry in Hunan Agricultural University. His research interests include Biosensing
Jiangbo Yang is pursuing his M.Sc. degree in Chemical Engineering in Hunan Agricultural University. His research interests include heavy metal electroanalysis.
Hai Yang is a professor in Hunan Institute of Engineering. His research interests include control of organic pollutants.
Zhaohong Su is an associate professor in Hunan Agricultural University. His research interests include electroanalysis and biosensing.
